paper_title: A new coupled ice sheet/climate model: description and sensitivity to model physics under Eemian, Last Glacial Maximum, late Holocene and modern climate conditions

methods.method_name: Energy-Moisture Balance Model (EMBM) for Surface Mass Balance Calculation
methods.description: This method calculates the ice sheet surface mass balance (SMB) using a modified version of the UVic ESCM vertically integrated energy-moisture balance atmosphere model (EMBM) and a simple snow model. It is used to determine melt, runoff, sublimation, and accumulation on ice sheets.

steps.description: Inputs to the energy balance calculation are incoming shortwave radiation, specific humidity, wind speed, surface skin temperature, albedo and roughness, and surface air temperature (elevated from sea level using a constant lapse rate, nominally 5.0 • C km −1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare inputs for energy balance calculation

steps.description: The snow temperature is found by solving the energy balance equation for temperature, using Newton's method.
steps.searchable_summary: Solve energy balance equation for snow temperature using Newton's method

steps.description: During the iteration, the surface equilibrium vapor pressure is determined and compared to the ambient specific humidity.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine surface equilibrium vapor pressure and compare to ambient specific humidity

steps.description: If the equilibrium specific humidity is greater, solid-to-vapor wind-enhanced sublimation occurs to equalize vapor pressures and the outgoing latent heat and moisture fluxes are determined.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate sublimation and outgoing latent heat and moisture fluxes

steps.description: If the surface temperature is at the melting point after the iteration and a net flux of energy into the surface exists, it is entirely used to melt snow/ice.
steps.searchable_summary: Use net energy flux to melt snow/ice if surface temperature is at melting point

steps.description: An arbitrary maximum snow thickness of 10 m is prescribed, above which any additional accumulation is added to the ice sheet as a positive SMB.
steps.searchable_summary: Prescribe maximum snow thickness and calculate positive SMB

steps.description: The snow model is greatly simplified by assuming that snow/ice heat capacity c p = 0 for the sake of energy balance calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: Simplify snow model by assuming zero heat capacity

steps.description: To capture the effect of refreezing within the relatively simple snow model, the parameterization described in Pfeffer et al. (1991) for representing refreezing of meltwater is adopted.
Over ice sheets, surface albedo varies as a function of snow/ice thickness and snow/ice temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate surface albedo based on snow/ice thickness and temperature

steps.description: Moisture is delivered to the ice sheets via advective/diffusive transport within the atmospheric model.
steps.searchable_summary: Deliver moisture to ice sheets using atmospheric model

steps.description: Precipitation occurs when the relative humidity at the surface surpasses 0.85.
steps.searchable_summary: Trigger precipitation when surface relative humidity exceeds threshold

methods.method_name: Ice Sheet Evolution Sensitivity to Climate Model Surface Air Temperature Bias
methods.description: This method evaluates the impact of seasonally-resolved modelled temperature biases on surface mass balance (SMB) and long-term ice sheet evolution by comparing model simulations with and without bias correction.

steps.description: Compare the 1970-2001 UVic ESCM SAT with the equivalent ERA40 SAT field to determine the extent of the model SAT bias.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare UVic ESCM SAT with ERA40 SAT to assess model bias

steps.description: Compare the Eemian, LGM and late Holocene control (bias-corrected) simulations to similar simulations forced with raw UVic ESCM SAT.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare bias-corrected and non-bias-corrected simulations for different periods

steps.description: Use of a constant bias corrector field assumes that the modelled SAT bias is independent of the climate or ice sheet state.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply constant bias corrector field assuming independence from climate state
